title: GripCarStatus
description: A single point-in-time observation of one car's state — position, optional speed, optional heading, emergency flag, and timestamps. Producers (in-car devices) capture and send these; receivers (event control, rally management) ingest them. Two timestamp fields make ownership explicit: `clientCapturedAt` is set by the producer at sample time, `serverReceivedAt` is set by the ingest server on arrival. Either may be null. App-specific telemetry (g-forces, tyre pressures, drivetrain) lives under `extensions`.

extensions.description: App-specific namespaced data. Keys are app slugs (kebab-case recommended).

schemaVersion.description: Version of the car-status schema this document conforms to.

number.description: Competition number. String to permit alphanumeric entries (e.g. '0' or '12A').

id.description: Optional stable identifier for implementations that need it. Not required for exchange; the competition number is authoritative within an event.

clientCapturedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC instant the sample was captured on the in-car device. Set by the producer. Null if the device can't determine a reliable wall-clock time.

serverReceivedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC instant the sample was received by the ingest server. Set by the server on arrival; producers should leave this null.

altMeters.description: Altitude in metres above the WGS84 ellipsoid.

accuracyMeters.description: Estimated 1σ horizontal position accuracy in metres. Smaller is better; null when unknown.

source.description: Position source: GPS, dead-reckoning (no satellite signal), or hybrid sensor fusion (typical for in-car devices with both).

speedMetersPerSecond.description: Ground speed in metres per second. Null when unknown.

headingDegrees.description: Heading in degrees clockwise from true north. Null when unknown or stationary.

emergency.description: True if the car has signalled an emergency (e.g. SOS/OK button pressed). Receivers should treat as latched until the producer clears it with a follow-up sample carrying emergency:false. Distinct from `priority` — `emergency` is a state flag describing the car; `priority` is a transport-layer hint about this specific document.

priority.description: Optional transport-layer priority hint. Repeaters and low-bandwidth networks can use this to prioritize critical traffic (bump transmit power, allocate bandwidth). Producers should typically bump to 'emergency' when emergency=true, but the two fields are independent so the schema doesn't enforce a coupling.
